title: Camissonia parryi
taxonomy: Camissonia parryi belongs to the family Onagraceae, commonly known as the evening primrose family. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Camissonia. Camissonia parryi is a perennial plant that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is often found growing in sandy or gravelly areas, including washes and dry lake beds. Camissonia parryi is known by several other names, including Parry's evening primrose and Parry's suncup.
uses: I found some information on Camissonia parryi, but its medicinal uses are not widely documented. parts: Camissonia parryi contains the roots and stems as its medicinal parts, which have been traditionally used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. preparations: Camissonia parryi, also known as Parry's sand verbena, has been traditionally used in herbal preparations for its medicinal properties. intro: Camissonia parryi, also known as Evening Primrose, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Onagraceae family, characterized by its delicate white flowers that bloom in the evening and produce a sweet fragrance. The health benefits of Camissonia parryi include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, skin conditions, and other inflammatory diseases. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Camissonia parryi is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often marketed to support skin health, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
